denial_pattern_sources: peer_to_peer_window_verbal_only: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; documented in multiple state PA reform laws (TX HB 2002, VA SB 1272)
algo_time_delta: ProPublica + Capitol Forum Cigna Investigation 2023; Senate Finance Committee UHC Investigation 2024
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 08/10/2026 for Continuous glucose monitoring during pregnancy related to Gestational diabetes on insulin (O24.414). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Cigna Medical Coverage Policies and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG). The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The requested frequency exceeds plan guidelines for Gestational diabetes on insulin (O24.414). Additional visits are not medically necessary at this cadence.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with frequency excessive. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Gestational diabetes on insulin (O24.414). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Continuous glucose monitoring during pregnancy recommended as the next appropriate step. Postprandial glucose >140 mg/dL on insulin; hypoglycemia episodes. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that frequency excessive bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Cigna correspondence with MCG Care Guidelines and procedural tone.
